name: Freddie Hubbard
abstract: Frederick Dewayne Hubbard (April 7, 1938 – December 29, 2008) was an American jazz trumpeter. He played bebop, hard bop, and post-bop styles from the early 1960s onwards.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Freddie Hubbard had a strong influence on me, the way he played, his command of improvisation, his great virtuosity.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: So I joined Freddie’s band and I stayed in his band for about two and a half or three years
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: they played “Straight Life” of Freddie Hubbard. And it just blew me away.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: Freddie Hubbard and myself, we were very good friends. We were the same age. So we lived in the same building in Brooklyn... So that was something I really was enjoying doing at the time... we had this group we had he called The Communicators... Freddie and myself, we, over the years, we played toge
about_quotes.doc_title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: Art Blakey had a very special knack for uh, uh discovering people or, you know, remembering people, and sure enough, he called me and Freddie Hubbard. We went in the same day with the Mosaic that we mentioned earlier, that piece. And uh we had a great uh time in a, in that group
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I asked if I could sit in on drums. And Freddie I'd met in Chicago when he came through sometime with Art Blakey. I jammed with him and Reggie Workman so we weren't like strangers. So, Freddie let me sit it and he called 'Just One Of Those Things'
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
